/Script/Voxel.VoxelOutputNode_OutputHeig.HeightRange: Height will be clamped between Min and Max
/Script/Voxel.VoxelOutputNode_OutputHeig.RelativeHeightRange:
If true and the blend mode is set to Override, HeightRange will be set relative to the previous stamps heights
Use this if your override graph is offsetting the previous height
/Script/Voxel.VoxelOutputNode_OutputHeig.Alpha:
The weight with which to overlay this graph surface and metadatas, over top of the previous state.
0 means entirely the previous state, 1 means entirely this graph's state.
Used only with Override blend mode.

/Script/Voxel.VoxelOutputNode_OutputVolu.Alpha:
The weight with which to overlay this graph surface and metadatas, over top of the previous state.
0 means entirely the previous state, 1 means entirely this graph's state.
Used only with Override blend mode.

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Position: Position at which to calculate output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Jitter: How irregular the cells are, needs to be between 0 and 1 to avoid glitches
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Seed: Used to randomize the output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Value: Distance to nearest cell center at position

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Position: Position at which to calculate output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Jitter: How irregular the cells are, needs to be between 0 and 1 to avoid glitches
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Seed: Used to randomize the output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Cell.Value: Distance to nearest cell center at position

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_Powe.Base: If negative, abs(Base) will be used instead
If negative, abs(Base) will be used instead

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_True.SecondNeighborDistance: Distance to the second-closest neighbor edge
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_True.ThirdNeighborDistance: Distance to the third-closest neighbor edge

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_True.SecondNeighborDistance: Distance to the second-closest neighbor edge
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelComputeNode_True.ThirdNeighborDistance: Distance to the third-closest neighbor edge

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Position: Position at which to calculate output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Amplitude: Height difference of the lowest and highest point of the noise's largest octave
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.FeatureScale: Amount of space the noise will take to tile in the world, a divisor for position
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Lacunarity: A factor for how much smaller each octave's feature scale is compared to last octave's
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Gain: A factor for how much smaller each octave's amplitude is compared to last octave's
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.CellularJitter: Random offset applied to cell position when using cellular noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.NumOctaves: Amount of layers this noise should have
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Seed: Used to randomize the output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Value: Result of all octaves being added together

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Position: Position at which to calculate output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Amplitude: Height difference of the lowest and highest point of the noise's largest octave
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.FeatureScale: Amount of space the noise will take to tile in the world, a divisor for position
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Lacunarity: A factor for how much smaller each octave's feature scale is compared to last octave's
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Gain: A factor for how much smaller each octave's amplitude is compared to last octave's
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.CellularJitter: Random offset applied to cell position when using cellular noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.NumOctaves: Amount of layers this noise should have
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Seed: Used to randomize the output noise
/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_AdvancedNoi.Value: Result of all octaves being added together

/Script/VoxelGraph.VoxelNode_GetGradient.Granularity:
Sets how granular the result will be: if very small, will return the gradient very close to the surface
If bigger will give a broader gradient for that area
Get Gradient computes a delta between values at X + Granularity, X - Granularity, Y + Granularity etc

Sets how granular the result will be: if very small, will return the gradient very close to the surface
If bigger will give a broader gradient for that area
Get Gradient computes a delta between values at X + Granularity, X - Granularity, Y + Granularity etc

/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_Generate2DPoi.CellSize:
A point will be placed in every cell the surface intersects with
This is more or less the average distance between points

/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.UpdateRotation: If true, will set the new point rotation to match the distance field gradient at the new position
/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.ForceDirection:
If true, will look for a new surface in the point downward directly, according to its rotation
Ignored if sampling height layer
/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.KillDistance:
If a point is further away than this distance from the surface
at the end of the raymarching, its density will be set to 0
Ignored if sampling height layer
/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.Tolerance:
If distance to surface is less than this, stop the raymarching
Ignored if sampling height layer
/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.MaxSteps:
Max number of steps to do during raymarching
Ignored if sampling height layer
/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.Speed:
How "fast" to converge to the surface, between 0 and 1
NewPoint = OldPoint + DistanceToSurface * Direction * Speed
Decrease if the raymarching is imprecise
Ignored if sampling height layer
/Script/VoxelPCG.VoxelNode_RaymarchLayer.GradientStep: Distance between points when sampling gradients
